seoTitle: Expand & Collapse Column Headers in a Power BI Matrix
metaDescription: Learn how to expand and collapse column headers in a Power BI matrix visual to keep dense reports clean while still letting viewers drill into detail.
intro: Matrix visuals in Power BI can get cluttered fast once you add several columns of data. This tutorial shows how to turn on column header expand and collapse behavior so viewers can see a clean summary view first, then drill into detail only when they need it.

steps.heading: Build your matrix visual
steps.body: Start with a matrix visual in Power BI Desktop and add the fields you want summarized across rows and columns, such as regions or departments.

steps.heading: Enable the expand option
steps.body: Turn on the setting that lets column headers expand, revealing the underlying detail fields nested beneath a summary column.

steps.heading: Collapse for a cleaner view
steps.body: Collapse the same headers when you want to return to a high-level summary, keeping the report readable for stakeholders who just need the top-line numbers.

steps.heading: Apply it to real reporting scenarios
steps.body: Use this technique on reports like sales by region or department so users can toggle between summary and detail without duplicating visuals.

faq.question: Does expanding column headers change the underlying data?
faq.answer: No, it only changes how much detail is displayed. The underlying dataset and calculations stay the same.

faq.question: Can I set the matrix to load already expanded or collapsed?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set a default expand state so the report opens in the view your audience needs most often.

faq.question: Does this work with any matrix, or only certain data types?
faq.answer: It works with any matrix that has hierarchical or multi-level column fields, regardless of the underlying data type.

faq.question: Will this slow down my report's performance?
faq.answer: Expand and collapse is a display setting, not a query change, so it does not meaningfully affect performance.
